title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not tool speed, is the constraint limiting AI agent effectiveness in software development. He proposes a four-layer stack including signal agents to reduce context switching, verification gates for safety, weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history, and holistic well-being monitoring via wearables.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS lays out a four-layer system for asynchronous, AI-assisted development that lets agents run autonomously while keeping a human reachable. The layers are: signal agents for context management, verification gates for safety, self-improvement loops that analyze conversation history, and holistic monitoring such as sleep tracking to guard against burnout. The framing matters for anyone running agentic workflows outside the desk, where shipping continues without constant supervision. The takeaway is a concrete stack that pairs autonomous agent work with safety gates and feedback loops rather than treating agents as fire-and-forget.
